apiEvents.htmlDescription: Climate Day brings together the natural products industry to accelerate climate action and food system resilience. Through inspiring keynotes, education sessions and an interactive resource fair, attendees connect with industry leaders to discover the innovations, insights, and community partnerships needed to drive meaningful environmental and social impact.

withEvent.title: Climate Day

apiEvents.htmlDescription: In this opening keynote, Dr. Samuel Ramsey explores the intersection of nature and climate, using pollinators as a powerful lens for understanding how ecological health, food systems, and climate resilience are deeply interconnected. Drawing on his research and experience as an entomologist and science communicator, Dr. Ramsey will illuminate what pollinators reveal about the state of our ecosystems, why their vulnerability matters for agriculture and food security, and how protecting living systems is essential to building a more resilient climate future.

withEvent.title: Climate Day Welcome & Keynote: What Pollinators Reveal at the Intersection of Nature & Climate

apiEvents.htmlDescription:
Content, connections and inspiration for the conscious products community.

Innovation is accelerating at an unprecedented pace, and the CPG industry is at the center of it all—from AI-driven operations and next-gen marketing strategies to evolving retail models and impact-driven business approaches.

How can you be a part of the change, rather than just observe it? Enter Natural Products Expo West’s inaugural CPG Innovation Summit, where brands, investors, service providers and other leading innovators collide to learn, collaborate and forge the future. Taking place Tuesday, March 3 at the Anaheim Marriott—before the Expo West tradeshow floor opens—this Summit will gather attendees for a one-day journey centered around transforming today’s opportunities into impact and growth. 

What You'll Learn: 

  • Innovation Frameworks: Practical ways to leverage technology, consumer insights and conscious business models for competitive advantage.
  • Capital Acceleration: Curated opportunities for brands and investors to connect directly on innovation-focused growth.
  • Retail Models of Tomorrow: Insights into the evolving landscape of retail and distribution—and what it takes to succeed in it.

Experience An Action-Packed Agenda Featuring:

  • Inspiring and Actionable Keynotes and Panels
  • Interactive Workshops and Unique Networking Opportunities
  • Always-On Networking Lounge
  • Content Creation Studio
  • Investor and Brand Presentations

withEvent.title: CPG Innovation Summit

withEvent.title: CPG Innovation Summit: Morning Wellness Wakeup Call

apiEvents.htmlDescription:
As companies advance regenerative agriculture commitments, it is increasingly clear that meaningful progress depends on innovative partnerships across brands, farmers, and markets. Unlocking regenerative outcomes at scale requires moving beyond isolated pilots toward coordinated, long-term approaches that support farmers through transition.

This session explores how aligned brand partnerships can enable regenerative transition on the ground by centering farmer needs and building shared value across supply chains. The conversation will examine how these partnerships help resolve many of the core barriers faced during transition, including risk, cost, market uncertainty, and misaligned incentives.

withEvent.title: Climate Day: Unlocking a Constellation of Regeneration - Innovative Partnerships Enabling Regenerative Transition

apiEvents.htmlDescription:
In a world obsessed with disruption, a quieter revolution rooted in simplicity, authenticity and trust in underway. Across the CPG and retail landscape, brands and consumers are writing new rules of innovation. They’re seeking fewer ingredients and more meaning; less noise and more nourishment. This shift is redefining the future of consumption by getting back to basics: doubling down on transparent and responsible business models, time-honored traditions and authentic ways of leading.  

Join VP, Amazon Worldwide Grocery Stores, and CEO, Whole Foods Market Jason Buechel alongside author and food justice advocate Mark Bittman for a thought-provoking conversation that challenges how we think about innovation—what drives it, what sustains it and what it looks like when progress meets purpose. Together, they’ll explore how this “new simplicity” is shaping everything from consumer food preferences to CPG market positioning to retail strategy—and what it means for the next era of conscious business.

This is more than a conversation about food—it’s a call to rethink the foundations of innovation itself.

withEvent.title: CPG Innovation Summit Keynote: Reimagining Innovation for a Changing World - A Conversation with Jason Buechel & Mark Bittman

apiEvents.htmlDescription:
The functional beverage category has achieved high momentum in the market. The category’s growth trajectory reflects a fundamental shift in consumer expectations: Beverages are no longer just refreshment but targeted wellness solutions that can benefit health or even function like supplements. For wellness beverage brands and retailers alike, this shift presents substantial opportunities to capture consumer investment in products that deliver authentic, science-backed benefits while simultaneously addressing multiple wellness priorities.

In this dynamic session, hear the latest insights, trends and innovations from SPINS, Whole Foods Market and Advantage Solutions, and from rising and established beverage leaders like Lifeway Foods, Aonic and Lily of the Desert.

withEvent.title: Trends and Innovation in the Wellness Beverage Marketplace

apiEvents.htmlDescription:
This presentation will focus on an overview of TikTok Shop Health - key takeaways from 2025, key focus areas for 2026, incentives for joining TikTok Shop, halo effect learnings, brand case studies, and much more!


TikTok Shop welcomes only sellers, brands, and agencies to this event.

withEvent.title: From Content to Cart: Unlocking Growth on TikTok Shop

apiEvents.htmlDescription:
As climate impacts accelerate, companies are increasingly focused on where their actions can deliver the greatest near-term climate benefit. Short-lived climate pollutants, including methane and refrigerants, are responsible for a significant share of near-term warming and represent one of the fastest opportunities to reduce heat in the climate system.

This session explores why centering short-lived climate pollutants within corporate climate action strategies is critical now. The conversation will examine where SLCP emissions show up across food and grocery value chains, how companies are identifying priority intervention points, and what it takes to move quickly from analysis to action.

withEvent.title: Climate Day: Centering Short-Lived Climate Pollutants in Climate Action Strategy

apiEvents.htmlDescription:
In an era where technology and artificial intelligence are shaping the marketing landscape, how can brands remain authentically human and leverage cutting-edge tools and platforms? Standing out in CPG requires more than high-impact campaigns—it’s about taking a thoughtful and adaptive approach to doing business and connecting with your customers. Success lies in embracing the balance between AI-driven efficiencies and the irreplaceable power of human creativity, empathy and storytelling.

This interactive workshop will start a conversation about how brands leaders, marketers, and strategists can navigate the complexities of AI, while keeping their brands' humanity front and center. Join us to discover how to craft innovative, resonant messaging that not only drives sales but also builds lasting loyalty with retailers and consumers. From maximizing resources to amplifying your team’s unique strengths, this session will equip you with actionable strategies to thrive in today’s dynamic marketing environment.

withEvent.title: CPG Innovation Summit: Human-Centered Marketing in an AI-Driven World - How to Stand Out & Stay Relevant

apiEvents.htmlDescription:
Powered by Nutrition Capital Network (NCN), the leading community connecting investors with high-potential companies in health, wellness and CPG, this exclusive opportunity offers unmatched visibility and strategic connections for growth-focused brands and CPG investors seeking the next big thing.

Selected companies will have the chance to network with and present directly to a room of investors, gaining exposure to key decision-makers in the industry. Additionally, leading CPG and investment experts will share insights with attendees on scaling your business, emerging categories, and capital opportunities shaping the future of natural products.

By combining NCN’s proven investor-brand platform with the dynamic energy of Expo West, the Deal Room delivers a unique environment for fostering innovation, securing funding, and driving growth in the natural products sector.

Agenda

  • Jennifer Pfeifer, Nutrition Capital Network

9:40-9:55am The Investor Climate in 2026 & Beyond

  • Hector Saldivar, Tia Lupita

9:55-10:10am Structuring Capital for Smarter Growth in Natural CPG
Scaling a natural CPG brand requires more than a great product—it demands the right capital strategy at every stage of growth. 
  
In this fireside chat, Banc of California joins CPG founders and investors, to discuss structuring capital strategically—from early development and launch to rapid retail expansion and national scale. As velocity increases and inventory demands strain cash flow, brands need more than funding—they need a trusted working capital partner. 
  
The conversation will focus on aligning capital with stage-specific priorities, financing working capital across the full company lifecycle, and building the operational strength that supports sustainable growth. Attendees will gain real-world insights on funding strategy, avoiding common scaling pitfalls, and choosing a banking partner who understands the nuances of CPG. 
  
Designed for founders and investors navigating the next phase of expansion, this discussion offers a clear framework for scaling strategically, protecting equity, and building long-term enterprise value with confidence. 

  • Carlos Ramos, Middle Market Banking Group Leader  
  • Justin Nedelman, Pressed Juicery
  • John Coletta, Clover-Sonoma
  • Brandon Neumann, CAVU Consumer Partners

  • Aqua Theon
  • Leaven Foods
  • PWR^UP
  • Frankly So

Ever wonder what “smart money” really means? In today’s CPG industry, capital is more than a resource—it’s the key to driving innovation, growth and transformation. This session will dig into the dynamic strategies that industry leaders are using to scale their businesses, fuel acquisitions and redefine customer engagement. Featuring a panel of CPG leaders, we’ll explore how to structure deals that align with long-term goals, leverage funding for breakthrough innovation and expand into competitive markets. Through real-world case studies and actionable insights, you’ll learn how to build strong brand-investor relationships, deploy capital effectively and position your business for sustainable success.

  • Jennifer Pfeifer, New Hope Network
  • Morgan Zanotti, Waay | Primal Kitchen

apiEvents.htmlDescription:
Wellness used to be sold as aspiration—perfection, purity, optimization. Today, consumers want something far more human. They’re turning to brands that speak with honesty, humor, and empathy rather than promising an unattainable ideal.

In this candid conversation, marketing leaders from across the wellness landscape—from herbs to hydration—explore how their brands are redefining what “well” means in real life. Together, they’ll unpack how imperfection has become an unexpected brand asset, how transparency builds trust, and how cultural authenticity intersects with scientific credibility.

Panelists will share real-world moments when their brands chose authenticity over aspiration and how that choice reshaped audience connection. Attendees will walk away with insights on evolving brand language, creative storytelling, and campaign strategies that resonate with today’s more emotionally intelligent, self-aware consumers.

withEvent.title: The Great Wellness Rebrand: From Aspiration to Real Life

apiEvents.htmlDescription:
This fast-paced Lightning Round brings together short, focused updates on the ideas, innovations, and collaborations shaping climate action across the food and grocery sector. In seven five-minute talks, speakers will share timely perspectives on market conditions, policy developments, and real-world examples of action.

The session will cover macro-economic and market trends, emerging policy opportunities, applied case studies on packaging, waste reduction, and insetting, as well as new approaches to lifecycle assessment and regenerative collaboration. Together, these rapid-fire insights provide a clear view of what is shifting now and where momentum is building.

withEvent.title: Climate Day: Lightning Round - Critical Updates & Innovations Shaping Climate Action

apiEvents.htmlDescription:
In a moment marked by climate urgency, market pressure, and rapid change, some leaders continue to make climate action central to how they do business. In this C-suite panel, executives will reflect on why strong climate values have shaped their companies in the past, continue to guide decision-making today, and remain essential to their vision for the future.

The conversation will explore what it means to lead with conviction amid uncertainty, including how leaders stay grounded in values while navigating complexity and risk. Panelists will share how centering stewardship of people and planet is integral to building resilient businesses, strengthening trust, and supporting long-term growth.

withEvent.title: Climate Day: Leading with Conviction - How Strong Climate Values Can Drive Connection & Growth

apiEvents.htmlDescription:
As the women’s health market continues to grow and diversify product offerings, “the Queen of Ayurvedic herbs,” shatavari is poised to gain significant traction with Nutritional Business Journal projecting growth rates upward of 38% over the next four years. Helping to drive this growth is breakthrough branded ingredient SRI-81 Shatavari. Backed by years of research, SRI-81 has been clinically proven to offer a holistic intervention that supports through every phase of a women’s life.

In this session, led by renowned physician, educator and holistic medicine expert Dr. Low Dog, learn the rich traditional heritage and emerging clinical science of Shatavari, along with insights into how SRI-81 can serve as a foundation for innovation across multiple women’s wellness categories.

withEvent.title: Introducing Shatavari: The Next Breakthrough Ingredient For Women’s Health

apiEvents.htmlDescription:
Consumers are increasingly using AI to get recommendations for health products designed for “someone like me”, and expect measurable, personalized results. This requires brands to go beyond average outcomes to demonstrate precision efficacy—how and why a product works for specific populations. This panel brings together clinical research, R&D, and brand strategy experts to explore how technology and AI are transforming the way companies validate product benefits, identify ideal consumer segments, and retain loyal customers. From ingredient evaluation to real-world validation, the discussion will reveal how precision trials and AI-driven insights empower brands to create products that truly deliver for “someone like me.”

Attendees will learn how to:

  • Evaluate clinical evidence to understand which populations and endpoints truly demonstrate efficacy for your ingredient or formulation.
  • Design precision clinical trials that capture meaningful, subgroup-specific outcomes beyond the average response.
  • Leverage AI and data analytics to translate complex clinical findings into actionable consumer insights and targeted marketing strategies.
  • Enhance consumer retention and acquisition efficiency by aligning validated health benefits with the right audience segments.

withEvent.title: Precision Evidence: How AI & Clinical Trials are Redefining Effectiveness & Consumer Trust in Health Products

apiEvents.htmlDescription:
As climate impacts accelerate, vulnerabilities in food and agricultural supply chains are becoming increasingly visible, particularly at the crop level. Shifting weather patterns, extreme events, and ecological stress are already affecting yields, sourcing regions, and long-term availability, creating material risks for companies across the food system.

In this fireside chat, Sam Kass joins Julia Collins for a conversation grounded in emerging research on crop vulnerability and its implications for business leaders. Together, they will explore why climate risk must be treated as a core supply chain issue, how climate change is reshaping where and how food is grown, and what it will take to build resilience in the face of growing uncertainty. This session invites leaders to move from risk awareness to collective action, and to consider how collaboration across companies and sectors can help protect crops, strengthen supply chains, and build a more resilient food system.

withEvent.title: Climate Day Keynote: From Risk to Resilience - Rethinking Supply Chains for a Changing Climate

apiEvents.htmlDescription:
For years the natural products space has been a hotbed for beauty innovation, capturing market share as consumers shift away from conventional products. The latest numbers show just how significant this shift has become. Personal care is growing faster than any other category in the natural products industry, at 6.7% in 2024, reaching $21 billion in sales.

This session will examine key trends driving this transformation—such as beyond clean, beauty as wellness ritual and hormone-sensitive and sexual wellness products, while also addressing emerging trends. Hear from NIQ’s Sherry Frey, PCC Community Markets Steven Jamieson, along with rising and established brand leaders like Garden of Life, BOND, Clapoti and Empress.

withEvent.title: Trends and Innovation in the Conscious Beauty & Women’s Health Marketplace

apiEvents.htmlDescription:
Innovation isn’t just about doing better things – sometimes it’s doing things better. As CPG brands face an astonishing convergence of disruption (from human and non-human actors alike), it is more important than ever to understand how, and where, data can drive growth and innovation. 

Finding, activating, and creating durable relationships with shoppers has aways been priority #1 for CPG brands. But today, the pace, scale, and specificity of where and how that happens is ramping up. 

In this session – SPINS brings together three leaders to discuss how real-time, hyperlocal intelligence, commerce insights, and agentic technologies are transforming the way brands connect with consumers and reach the next horizon of growth. 

Learn practical strategies to turn data into action— building durable relationships with customers, optimizing investments in omnichannel experiences, and future-proofing your business in an increasingly complex marketplace.

withEvent.title: CPG Innovation Summit: Analytics, Activations, & Agents - Using Data to Survive & Thrive in a 3-Shelf Reality

The 2026 Climate Leaders Awards will spotlight companies that are helping shift our sector from being part of the climate problem to being part of the solution. In the spirit of the Climate Collaborative’s mission, these awards surface real-world climate solutions that inspire, inform, and accelerate action across the natural products industry.

2026 Climate Leaders Awards Categories

  • Regenerative Agriculture Leadership, advancing a resilient and thriving food system
  • Forest Action Leadership, protecting and restoring forest ecosystems within sourcing and supply chains
  • Super Pollutants (SLCP) Leadership, reducing methane, refrigerants, and other short-lived climate pollutants
  • Climate-aligned Product Innovation Leadership, bringing climate-aligned products to market in ways that meaningfully reduce climate impact
  • Climate Communications Leadership Award, communicating climate action with clarity, transparency, and integrity
  • Collective Action Leadership Award, working together to advance climate solutions that no single organization could achieve alone

*Full Details, including FAQs and judging criteria are available on the Climate Collaborative website. Questions? Email info@climatecollaborative.com.

apiEvents.htmlDescription:
The seed oil-free movement is quickly gaining momentum— but what’s truly behind the shift? This panel brings together leaders from across the value chain, plus an expert in market data, to explore the forces shaping the movement today and what lies ahead.

From ingredient sourcing to CPG’s and food service, the discussion will unpack why seed oil–free has moved from niche to mainstream (and which categories reflect the biggest shifts), how it affects sourcing and production throughout the supply chain, and how sustainability and third-party certifications factor into the conversation.

Our speakers will provide insights and examine consumer behavior, retail trends, formulation challenges, and the future role of alternative fats and oils.

Attendees will leave with a clearer understanding of what’s fueling the seed oil–free movement today—and how brands, retailers, and ingredient suppliers can navigate what comes next.

withEvent.title: The Rise of the Seed Oil–Free Movement: What’s Driving It—and What Comes Next

apiEvents.htmlDescription:
This interactive workshop will equip attendees with a practical understanding of physical climate risks and the tools to identify and assess these risks within their business operations and value chains, helping them build a compelling business case for action. Participants will learn to distinguish between physical and transition risks and connect them directly to core business functions such as operations, finance, and supply chain management, reinforcing why climate risk is a strategic issue rather than solely an environmental one.

Using Probable Futures’ open-source mapping tool, attendees will receive a live demonstration and then apply the tool to screen for potential physical climate risks across their own company’s assets and supply chains. The session will include small-group discussions to translate findings into clear business implications.

Participants will leave with concrete skills to conduct a preliminary physical climate risk assessment and to articulate how these risks affect business strategy, operations, and financial performance in ways that leadership can understand and act on.

withEvent.title: Climate Day Workshop: Navigating Climate Risk with Open-Source Tools

apiEvents.htmlDescription:
Join WISEcode for a one-hour, high-signal session at the CPG Innovation Summit exploring how AI-powered food intelligence is reshaping product strategy, retail placement, and investment decisions across the industry.

New transparency regulations are coming. Retailers are rethinking shelf strategy. And the brands that will win both shifts already know exactly what’s in their products, what’s in their competitors’ products, and how to prove the difference.

Most brands don’t. AI just changed that.

WISEcode CEO Peter Castleman and Chief Product Officer Chris Knoch sit down with Scott Marcus, CEO of Blue Zones Kitchen; and Elly Truesdell of New Fare Partners, the venture firm behind Bachan’s, for a candid conversation on how leading brands and investors are using ingredient-level intelligence to get ahead.

In this session, you will hear how brands are using ingredient-level benchmarking to outposition competitors without changing a single SKU, why investors are now screening portfolios through a processing and transparency lens before writing checks, and what retailers are beginning to demand that most brands are not yet equipped to deliver.

One hour. No fluff. The intelligence advantage is real, and it is moving fast.

withEvent.title: CPG Innovation Summit: From Labels to Leverage - How AI Turns Ingredient Data into Competitive Advantage

apiEvents.htmlDescription:
What's at the heart of every great innovation strategy? The consumer. 

Join Rachel Ferdinando, Chief Executive Officer of PepsiCo Foods U.S., for a behind-the-scenes look at how consumer insights drive breakthrough innovations in today's rapidly evolving CPG landscape. During this fast-paced keynote, Rachel will dive deep into PepsiCo's strategic decision-making process, revealing how consumer insights and retail relationships translate into business strategy across three critical innovation areas: creating cleaner, more permissible products that meet today's transparency demands; developing functional foods that deliver the nutrition benefits consumers actively seek; and ensuring accessibility to better-for-you options during challenging economic times.

See strategy in action as Rachel shares real frameworks for building strong customer partnerships,  insight into what works (and what doesn't) and the complex process of transforming consumer voices into products that win at retail. Discover how one of the world’s largest food and beverage companies navigates the intersection of consumer research, business strategy and purpose-driven innovation—and walk away with actionable advice for driving the next generation of CPG innovation that delivers scalable impact.

withEvent.title: CPG Innovation Summit Keynote: From Insight to Impact - How Consumer Voices Shape the Future of CPG Innovation

apiEvents.htmlDescription: Our Tuesday evening celebration returns for its second year with a special 45th anniversary "Through the Decades" theme. Get ready for a night of nostalgia, networking, and pure fun that honors our industry's remarkable evolution!

withEvent.title: Through the Decades Block Party

apiEvents.htmlDescription:
A Climate Transition Action Plan (CTAP) is a framework that guides business planning and responds to growing stakeholder expectations around a central question: How is your business working to achieve a net-zero future? Between public ESG reporting, local and national regulations, and supplier and customer requests, many companies already share elements of how they plan to move from setting greenhouse gas (GHG) reduction targets to taking action. However, few companies have taken the step of fully taking stock of their climate initiatives and formalizing them into a comprehensive CTAP that meets evolving stakeholder and regulatory requirements.

This session will provide actionable insights into how businesses, regardless of size, can assess their existing climate programs, disclosures, and initiatives to develop a robust CTAP that supports their overall climate strategy. Participants will leave with a clear understanding of how to leverage current resources and climate-related data to create a structured plan that addresses near-term priorities while supporting long-term sustainability goals.

withEvent.title: Climate Day Workshop: Developing a Corporate Climate Transition Action Plan (CTAP)

This session tackles the urgent challenge of financing smallholder farmer transitions to organic and regenerative agriculture in emerging markets, where the costs of certification, technical assistance, and inputs often fall on those least able to afford them. Speakers will draw on real-world case studies, including work with more than 1,000 smallholder farmers and leadership from the Regenerative Organic Certified community, to map out the spectrum of financing mechanisms that support transition.

The conversation will explore approaches including grants, equity, concessional debt, blended finance, and purchase order funding, along with the practical tradeoffs of each. Attendees will gain lessons from the field on structuring farmer-first models, de-risking adoption through input credit and technical assistance, and building farmer creditworthiness over time.

The session will also examine what comes next as bilateral aid and USAID funding recede, highlighting emerging funds and financing approaches that can lower the cost of capital for those closest to smallholder farmers, prevent backsliding into conventional practices, and keep farmer-led climate transitions moving forward.

The natural products industry was born from a love for the Earth and the healing power of plants. Yet, as we grow, it becomes easier to lose sight of that original connection. This session is a call to return to the source—to the forest, to the land, and to the spirit that guides our work. As an Anishinaabe business owner, I have learned that when you listen, the forest speaks. It offers wisdom not just for our products, but for how we live and how we run our businesses.

This presentation is an invitation to walk a path of deeper respect and reciprocity with Mother Earth. I will share personal stories from my time in the deep woods, where the air is different and the world reveals its secrets. We will explore how Indigenous wisdom, built on millennia of observation and connection, can guide the natural products industry toward a truly regenerative future. This is not just about sustainable sourcing; it’s about a relationship. It's about understanding that when you do things in a good way, good things will come back to you and your business. Attendees will leave with a renewed sense of purpose and a practical understanding of how to honor their connection to nature in every aspect of their work.

Attendees will walk away with:

  • A Framework for Reciprocity: Learn how to apply the Indigenous principle of reciprocity to business practices, creating a balanced relationship with suppliers, the environment, and customers.
  • Actionable Regenerative Practices: Discover practical methods for sourcing and production that go beyond sustainability to actively heal and regenerate the ecosystems we depend on.
  • A Deeper Connection to Your Mission: Reignite the passion for natural products by understanding the spiritual and energetic connection between plants, people, and the planet.
  • Guidance from the Forest: Gain insights into how to listen to and learn from nature, allowing it to inform business decisions and foster innovation.

To learn more, visit www.herballodge.com.

withEvent.title: Reconnecting to the Forest: An Indigenous Perspective on Regenerative Practices in the Natural Products Industry

apiEvents.htmlDescription:
The UC Davis Education Series, in partnership with SCIFTS, is a dynamic, multi-part seminar featuring leading experts discussing groundbreaking advancements that are shaping the future of food science and technology.

This year, hear from:

  • David Mills, Ph.D., Distinguished Professor & Peter J. Shields Chair in Dairy Food Science
  • Maria Marco, Ph.D., Professor & Chair of the Food Science Graduate Group
  • Carolyn Slupsky, Ph.D., Professor Food Science, Technology & Nutrition | Kinsella Endowed Chair in Food, Nutrition, & Health

Milk is Smart! Gut Microbe Metabolism of Milk Glycans Influences Brain Health

Dr. Mills studies the molecular biology and ecology of bacteria that play an active role in gut health and fermented foods and beverages. Human milk is well known to drive infant brain development. However, the mechanisms by which specific milk components accomplish this are poorly understood. New research at UC Davis shows that milk glycans help protect the developing neonate brain from inflammation, opening new strategies to safeguard and enhance cognition in both vulnerable infants and adults.

Understanding & Optimizing Fermented Foods for Improving Health

Dr. Marco investigates microorganisms in foods and intestinal ecosystems. This research emphasizes lactic acid bacteria and contributions of these bacteria to the production of fermented foods
and beverages and to human health and well-being. She and her team use systems biology, genetics, and ecology to study the unique properties of individual strains, as well as the functional capacities of food and gut microbiomes.

Fermented foods are frequently regarded as superfoods which support and improve health. This talk will discuss the state of human studies on fermented foods and the underlying principles guiding how the bioactive properties of fermented foods differ from the ingredients used to make them. By understanding the microbial variation and functional diversity of fermented foods in relation to their health-modulatory effects, it will be possible to design and optimize these foods for desired health outcomes.

Food as Medicine Across the Lifespan

Dr. Slupsky’s research studies the nexus between food, the gut microbiome, and health, with a focus on understanding how diet and the environment impact the relationship between the gut microbiome and host metabolism. She is particularly interested in how food / nutrition and environmental exposures impact the microbiome to shape prenatal and postnatal infant and child development. Dr. Slupsky brings deep expertise in relating diet to changes in the human gut microbiome and, by extension, human metabolism. Novel insights on health management and food development to usher in an era of personalized nutrition.

withEvent.title: UC Davis Education: Dynamic New Trends & Benefits from Food Fermentation, Probiotics, & Prebiotics

Sequoias can grow more than 300 feet tall, yet their roots reach only about 12 feet deep. Their strength comes not from depth, but from connection—their roots are intertwined. Their resilience is collective.

This image captures the core premise of the Sustainable Herbs Initiative (SHI) and a growing movement within the natural products industry: building regenerative supply networks where resilience comes from collaboration, transparency, and trust.

Resilience isn’t achieved by isolated action—it’s built through interconnected relationships that strengthen the whole ecosystem. This panel will explore how regenerative principles can guide how we work together, not just what we source or produce.

Panelists will share case studies and insights, including:
1.    Foundational principles on which the SHI network is based and the practices through which relationships among participants are established and deepened.
2.    New collaborative frameworks for mapping and reducing Scope 3 emissions across shared supply networks.
3.    Partnering—even among competitors—to address extreme climate disruptions from Hurricane Helene and to secure long-term ingredient supply.
4.    Supplier relationships grounded in reciprocity and shared value, rather than extraction and transaction.

Attendees will leave with practical examples and frameworks for building regenerative networks that drive both business resilience and meaningful climate action.

apiEvents.htmlDescription:
Most companies rely on Life Cycle Assessments (LCAs) to quantify and report the environmental impact of their products—from farm to shelf. These tools are foundational for corporate sustainability strategies, carbon accounting, and ESG reporting. But LCAs were originally designed for industrial production systems like factories, and when they were later adapted for agriculture, the models and assumptions were largely based on simplified, conventional systems. The result? More biologically complex and diversified agricultural systems—including organic, regenerative, perennial, and other ecologically complex production models—can be undervalued or misrepresented in corporate climate metrics.

  • Why LCAs—and the assumptions behind them—matter for every company making sourcing, investment, or climate decisions.
  • What’s at stake for organic, regenerative, and other non-conventional supply chains if these tools remain misaligned with ecological realities.
  • How more accurate, fit-for-purpose methods can improve credibility, comparability, and decision-making across the food industry.
  • Opportunities for collaboration: from data sharing and case studies to advancing more scientifically grounded measurement and reporting approaches.
  • Attendees will walk away with a clearer understanding of how measurement drives market perception, investment, and policy—and why it’s critical that the metrics used to assess complex agricultural systems are as robust and nuanced as the systems themselves.

withEvent.title: Getting Measurement Right: Improving How We Measure & Report on Complex Agricultural Systems

apiEvents.htmlDescription:
Many brands launch their products using thin supply chains—using a specialty ingredient or processing and ingredient in a new way. In fact, it’s central to their differentiation. But you can’t scale without a secure supply chain. Many brands start that journey by searching for a farmer.

That can work, but it also could be a disaster for you and the farmer.

Scaling organic and regenerative acreage depends on working through processors, mills, aggregators, and cooperatives that coordinate planting, pricing, logistics, and risk.

In this candid conversation, farmers, processors, and founders will unpack:

  • Who should be your first call? Meet your organic trade associations
  • Why starting with finding a processor dramatically increases your odds of success
  • How processors help align volume, rotation planning, and risk-sharing
  • What “consistent customer at a consistent price” really means to a farmer
  • How shared-risk pricing models unlock acreage
  • How "Farmer First Organic Pricing” will grow organic acreage and secure your supply
  • What brands misunderstand about payment timing and capital when working with farmers
  • Should you become a supplier or processor yourself to strengthen the entire chain

If your brand depends on organic, regenerative, or specialty crops, this session will give you a practical roadmap for building supply the right way — from the middle out.

withEvent.title: Don’t Let Your Unique Ingredients Prevent You from Scaling: The Overlooked Keys to Scaling Organic & Regenerative Supply for Your Brand

Artificial intelligence (AI) tools are revolutionizing the export landscape for tangible goods, such as food, nutritional supplements, and cosmetics. These innovative solutions can help you find new markets, identify suppliers and buyers, conduct a market analysis, and outline a global competitive strategy.

However, incorporating AI into your export business does not come without risks. Concerns about information accuracy and data privacy must be addressed to safeguard your business. This is where simple, practical tips that you can implement immediately become invaluable.

Join Sarita D. Jackson, PhD, Founder and CEO of the Global Research Institute of International Trade (GRIIT), for a non-technical, impactful session that will help your company to effectively and safely leverage AI. She will discuss the opportunities presented by AI while laying out a comprehensive safety plan to ensure smooth integration into your export processes.

After attending this session, you will be able to do the following:

  • Identify the various AI tools that can assist with your export needs;
  • List the skills required to make AI work for your company;
  • Create a plan to incorporate AI into your export process; and
  • Execute a simple step-by-step guide to managing AI-related risks.

Do not miss the chance to grow your business in a competitive global digital economy. Take the first step toward safely incorporating AI tools as you grow your exports.

The Global Export series, hosted by the U.S. Commercial Service and International Trade Administration, will take place on Wednesday, March 4th in the Global Resource Center, Room 204C, at the Anaheim Convention Center.  Attendance is included with exhibit hall pass.

withEvent.title: Using AI to Grow Your Export Business—Safely and Simply

apiEvents.htmlDescription:
Gary Vaynerchuk is a serial entrepreneur and serves as the Chairman of VaynerX, the CEO of VaynerMedia, and the CEO and creator of VeeFriends. Gary is considered one of the leading global minds on what's next in culture, business, and the internet. 

Known as "GaryVee," he is described as one of the most forward thinkers in business. He acutely recognizes trends and patterns early to help others understand how shifts in consumer attention impact the realities of the business world today. Gary's approach sits at the intersection of business and pop culture. He keenly understands how to bring brand relevance to the forefront. He is a prolific angel investor with early investments in companies like Facebook, Twitter, Tumblr, Venmo, Snapchat, Coinbase, and Uber.

Gary is an entrepreneur at heart – he builds businesses. Today, he helps Fortune 1000 brands leverage consumer attention through his full-service advertising agency, VaynerMedia, which has offices in New York, Los Angeles, Chicago, Miami, Toronto, Mexico City, London, Amsterdam, Sydney, Singapore, Tokyo, Bangkok, Delhi, and Kuala Lumpur. VaynerMedia is part of the VaynerX holding company, which also includes Eva Nosidam Productions, Gallery Media Group, The Sasha Group, VaynerSpeakers, VaynerCommerce, and Tingley Lane Trading. Gary is the Co-Founder of VaynerSports, VCR Group, VaynerWatt, ArtOfficial, Resy, and Empathy Wines. He guided Resy and Empathy to successful exits -- which he later sold to American Express and Constellation Brands, respectively. He also owns a Major League Pickleball team called the 5s, is part owner of a Big3 basketball team, and is an investor in the revival of the SlamBall League.

In 2021, Gary created VeeFriends, an entertainment company that has become a rising powerhouse in modern entertainment and collectibles. Often described as Pokemon meets Sesame Street, the company leverages stories, games, events, collectibles, and technology to scale its character universe. Vaynerchuk also has negotiated partnerships with brand powerhouses such as Crocs, Fanatics, Macy’s/Toys “R” Us, Mattel’s UNO, Mattel’s Masters of the Universe, Moonbug Entertainment, Reebok, Squishmallows, Topps, and more.

Gary is also the founder and creator of VeeCon – a contemporary super conference that converges business and pop culture with innovation and technology. He is a six-time New York Times bestselling author, with titles including Crush It!, Jab, Jab, Jab, Right Hook, Twelve and a Half and Day Trading Attention. In addition to running multiple businesses, Gary documents his daily life as a CEO through his social media channels, which have more than 45 million followers and garner more than 300 million monthly impressions/views across all platforms. His podcast, "The GaryVee Audio Experience," ranks among the top podcasts globally.

Gary serves on the board of MikMak, Bojangles Restaurants, Global Citizen Forum and Pencils of Promise. He is also a longtime Well Member of charity: water.

Gary's life ambition is to buy the New York Jets.

withEvent.title: Thursday Keynote: Gary Vaynerchuk

Clean beauty isn’t just about what’s not in the bottle. Explore how efficacy and transparency are reshaping shopper behaviors and loyalty, informed by new research conducted and jointly presented by Acosta Group and NielsenIQ (NIQ).  

A new shopper study reveals a powerful duality in today’s health and beauty shopper: while ingredient transparency is a key purchase driver, product performance often rises to the top for decision-making — especially in general beauty and personal care items. In skincare, consumers are evenly split between prioritizing clean, natural ingredients and visible results, underscoring the need for brands and retailers to strike a balance in communicating efficacy and transparency. 

This session also spotlights the allergy-conscious consumer, a highly engaged and label-aware segment. Acosta Group research finds that 25% of shoppers now check health and beauty labels due to personal or family food allergies, sensitivities, or reactions. Learn which products attract these consumers, where they're conducting their research, and how brands can better serve this informed, growing, and loyal audience.

withEvent.title: Beyond the Label: Balancing Ingredient Integrity with Product Performance

Learn practical, immediate steps to democratize your brand data and strategically craft content that satisfies the algorithms of tomorrow, ensuring your product is found, understood, and purchased in the new retail ecosystem.

apiEvents.htmlDescription:
Despite being made with nutrient-dense, whole food ingredients and time-tested culinary traditions, international cuisines have long been excluded from the mainstream wellness conversation. Instead, they’re often siloed to the “international aisle,” while similar foods rooted in more familiar American palates are more likely to be positioned as “natural” or “healthy.”

According to Mintel, more than a quarter of consumers are interested in tasting products with international flavors. As consumers’ appetite for globally inspired ingredients continues to grow, so does the global natural and organic food industry, which is projected to have a CAGR of 14% from 2024 to 2030. Recent cultural zeitgeists reflect an emerging convergence, from the matcha explosion to saffron’s rise as a mood-boosting supplement. 

This panel brings together founders who are redefining what health food looks and tastes like. They’ll discuss how their brands are breaking through cultural and retail stereotypes, educating consumers about the inherent wellness benefits of global cuisines, and positioning their products to resonate with health-minded shoppers seeking authenticity, flavor, and function.

withEvent.title: Breaking Through Stereotypes: The Future of Health Food is Global Flavor

• What true omnichannel strategy looks like in today’s retail environment.

• Approaches for leveraging data and media to create seamless, shopper-first experiences.

withEvent.title: Fermentation for a Better Tomorrow: Unlocking Nature’s Potential

apiEvents.htmlDescription:
Today’s food market is more health-focused than ever, creating new opportunities and challenges for companies competing on health. In the plant-based category, understanding how different consumers define “healthy” and how much it influences their choices is increasingly critical to growth.

In this session, GFI will share fresh consumer and category insights on how health drives value in today’s plant-based space, paired with candid perspectives from brands in the category on product decisions and messaging choices—including what’s worked and what hasn’t—in a rapidly evolving, health-forward landscape.

withEvent.title: Competing on Health: Insights for Plant-Based Brands

apiEvents.htmlDescription:
92% of food and beverage sales still happen in-store. Yet Amazon commands roughly 40% of ecommerce sales - while capturing over 75% of retail media budgets. These two stats alone represent a profound allocation gap that leaves millions in incremental sales growth for CPG brands completely untapped.

In this session, Eleanor Hayden, founder and CEO of Veriti, will unveil new research from Forrester on where shoppers are truly buying and the media strategies that drive authentic profitability, not just ROAS and YoY sales growth. She'll expose the imbalance between spend and shopper behavior, and quantify the risks of imbalance in ecommerce, retail media, and shopper marketing advertising investments. She'll also bring these truths to life through real stories from emerging CPG brands: how they secure meaningful placements that compete with enterprise budgets, unlock new-to-brand growth (especially on platforms like Instacart and DoorDash), and drive incremental profit through some of the most underrated online and in-store marketing tools. She’ll close with three strategies attendees can immediately deploy to rebalance their budgets, integrate in-store and retail media, and actually grow sales - not just impressions.

Attendees will leave with:

  • A clarified view of where retail media spend is misaligned with actual buyer behavior
  • Real-world examples of profits unlocked when in-store marketing is layered with retail media
  • Three actionable moves to improve velocity, drive incrementality, and future-proof budgets in 2025 and beyond

withEvent.title: Rewriting the Retail Media Playbook: In-Store, Incrementality & Real CPG ROI

apiEvents.htmlDescription:
As concern about ultra-processed foods intensifies, leaders across the natural products industry are grappling with the same question: what comes next? Moving beyond ingredients alone, this session examines how processing itself is shaping health outcomes, consumer trust, and the future of food.

Bringing together a physician, a leading natural brand executive, a market researcher, and certification leadership, the panel connects emerging health research with national consumer insights and on-the-ground brand experience. The conversation explores how shoppers are interpreting ultra-processing, how expectations are shifting for brands and retailers, and what practical pathways exist for reformulation, transparency, and credible verification.

Attendees will leave with a clearer understanding of the science, the market signals, and the real-world decisions facing brands and retailers — along with insight into how collaborative, non-UPF approaches can support innovation, rebuild trust, and help the natural products industry lead what comes next.

withEvent.title: Ultra-Processing & What Comes Next: Reshaping Trust, Health, & the Future of Natural Food

apiEvents.htmlDescription: Every functional ingredient has a flavor sweet spot. In this session, discover how food, beverage and supplement brands are using Kerry’s Taste Charts to pair protein, fiber, and next-gen functional ingredients with flavors that feel intentional, not forced. Walk away with insights on building functional foods and beverages that resonate from first sip to last bite.

withEvent.title: Function, Meet Your Flavor Match

apiEvents.htmlDescription: Health and wellness is experiencing explosive growth—yet over 50% of consumers say food and beverages aren’t delivering. Why—and what can brands do to turn this around? In this session, Dairy Management Inc. is joined by Black Swan Data, a Mintel company, to show how social and cultural intelligence can be used to go deeper into consumer needs—revealing the benefit bundles consumers are seeking, the emerging needs shaping the future, and how to deliver against them. This breakthrough work draws on insights from >200 million online conversations captured by Black Swan Data, >14,000 ZS surveys, and Euromonitor analytics.

withEvent.title: What Consumers Really Want—And Emerging Ways To Deliver It

apiEvents.htmlDescription:
In an era where functional mushroom information is increasingly shaped by social media sound bites and viral misinformation, this session brings science back to the center of the conversation.

This panel features Paul Stamets-Founder, Member, and Owner of Fungi Perfecti, LLC; Dr. Julie Daoust, Chief Science and Technology Officer at M2 Ingredients and Chair and Co-Founder of the Functional Mushroom Council; Dr. Chase Beathard, PhD, MBA, Director of Research and Development at Fungi Perfecti, LLC; and Shane Heath, Founder and CEO of MUD/WTR.

Paul Stamets will discuss a breakthrough, peer-reviewed study conducted by researchers at the University of California San Diego and Fungi Perfecti, LLC—a placebo-controlled human clinical trial highlighting mushroom mycelium as a well-tolerated approach to supporting immune resilience and immune memory during routine immune stress.

Dr. Daoust and Dr. Beathard will present new and emerging science on finished functional mushroom products, including research that evaluates real world formulations rather than isolated compounds or borrowed literature. Their discussion will highlight what the data actually shows about ingredient quality, formulation decisions, and the value of full spectrum mushroom ingredients when assessed through modern scientific methods.

Building on this scientific foundation, Shane Heath will share how brands can responsibly translate complex research into compelling, consumer-friendly education. Drawing from MUD/WTR’s experience, he will discuss how to use social media as a tool for transparency and trust building, while actively debunking common myths and misinformation that dominate short form content platforms.

Together, the panel connects rigorous science with practical communication, offering attendees a clear framework for evaluating mushroom ingredients, understanding new research, and educating buyers and consumers without oversimplification or exaggeration. The session emphasizes collaboration across science, supply chain, and brand leadership as the path forward for a more credible and resilient functional mushroom industry.

withEvent.title: Science That Sells Without Compromise: Building Trust in Functional Mushroom Products

apiEvents.htmlDescription:
With 33 million Americans living with life-threatening food allergies—and nearly 85 million impacted when including caregivers and households—the food allergy community represents one of the fastest-growing, yet most underserved, consumer segments in the U.S. Despite this scale and purchasing power, many brands and retailers are only beginning to understand how to meaningfully meet their needs.

Join a diverse panel of industry and advocacy leaders as they explore the food allergy consumer perspective and what it truly takes to earn their trust. The discussion will focus on transparency, safety, and inclusive nutrition innovation—along with practical strategies for driving incremental sales and building lasting brand loyalty within this highly engaged consumer base.

Moderated by Tyler Livingston, RD, Director of Certified Free From Allergens, this panel will feature insights from:

  • Emily Arthurs, Senior Director & General Manager of Lorissa’s Kitchen / LK Snacks — sharing the brand perspective on building trust and loyalty with allergy-aware consumers.
  • Mike Flot, Scientific Advisor and Food Safety Consultant — bringing a food safety perspective on allergen management and risk reduction that supports real transparency.
  • Louis Giorgi, Jr., M.D., Founder of the Natalie Giorgi Sunshine Foundation — representing the lived experience and voice of the food allergy community.
  • Becca Williams, President of Red Plate Foods — translating trust into product development, transparent communication and sustained growth within the allergy-aware market.

withEvent.title: Understanding & Winning Over the Food Allergy Consumer

apiEvents.htmlDescription:
In today’s digital-first world, a single viral moment can put an ingredient or product on the map, but not every trend has staying power. How can brands distinguish between short-lived buzz and long-term opportunity? In this session, Spate will team up with MaryRuth’s to explore the path from virality to vitality: how online curiosity and social chatter evolve into sustainable consumer demand. Spate will share digital insights from Google Search, TikTok, and Instagram that reveal how consumers discover and adopt wellness trends, while MaryRuth’s will bring the brand perspective — how they’ve navigated viral moments, innovated around digital signals, and built trust with consumers along the way. Together, the speakers will provide a data-meets-brand view of what it takes to transform digital culture into real business growth.
 
Attendees will:

  • Understand how viral trends emerge in search and social data from Google Search, TikTok, and Instagram.
  • Learn strategies to evaluate whether a viral trend has long-term potential.
  • Gain insights from MaryRuth’s on how brands can authentically engage with digital culture.
  • Walk away with a framework for connecting digital buzz to innovation and sustained consumer demand.

withEvent.title: Virality to Vitality: How Digital Platforms Drive Wellness Discovery

apiEvents.htmlDescription:
Take a midday coffee break to pause, sip, connect, and grow. This interactive networking session for women and allies is designed to foster meaningful connections, mentorship opportunities, and a supportive community. Through moments of mindfulness, discussion, gentle movement, and storytelling, we’ll explore the power of women supporting women. 

Whether you’re seeking guidance, offering mentorship, or simply looking to expand your network, this session is a fantastic opportunity to build new relationships at Expo West and access year-round support systems through communities such as the OSC Women's Circles.

withEvent.title: Crowdsourced Wit & Wisdom for Everyday Survival & Success: Women in CPG Networking Event

apiEvents.htmlDescription:
By 2030 over 30 million Americans may interact with a GLP-1 agonist medication as a tool for weight loss, blood sugar, inflammation, cognitive health and more. It's already having a ripple effect on consumer health, healthcare, food and ingredient industries. 

But the BIGGER story? These medications are introducing everyone to weight-health hormones. That these hormones regulate weight and health. That to enjoy weight health, today and tomorrow, a person needs to optimize their weight-health hormones... and the number of Americans that this impacts? 9 out of 10! 

What does this mean for the food, supplement, healthcare industries and the media? This session will provide insights, strategies, and guardrails for navigating weight health in the era of GLP-1. 

*Following the presentation, Ashley will do a book signing! Bring your own copy to be signed - plus, the first 20 people in line to attend this session will receive a free copy of YOUR BEST SHOT.

withEvent.title: How to Optimize Weight Health in the GLP-1 Era

apiEvents.htmlDescription:
Inflation aftershocks, elevated interest costs, and shifting household priorities are reshaping how consumers make everyday food decisions. In 2026, shoppers won’t simply trade down—they will value-flex, reallocating dollars across dayparts, channels, and occasions. This session explores how budget pressures are driving behavioral swaps such as replacing meals with snacks, skipping beverages, desserts, or appetizers, and restructuring family feeding routines both at home and in restaurants. We will decode where consumers stretch, where they economize, and where they still permit small, meaningful indulgences. The focus moves beyond pricing mechanics to the food and beverage implications: flavor systems, format innovation, bundled offerings by need state (Strong Start mornings, family take-home, snack-first occasions), and menu design that protects entry points while earning selective trade-ups. Attendees will leave with clear direction on how to align flavor, portioning, and format strategy with today’s financial realities without sacrificing excitement or margin resilience.

This session will:

  1. Decode Financial Behavior Shifts: Identify where consumers are swapping meals for snacks, trimming add-ons (beverages, desserts, appetizers), or restructuring family food routines—and what these behaviors signal for 2026 menu strategy.
  2. Translate Value-Flex into Food & Beverage Design: Apply consumer drivers to flavor systems, portion architecture, and bundled formats that protect entry price while enabling practical indulgence.
  3. Design for Real-World Feeding Needs: Build occasion-based solutions (Strong Start mornings, snack-first afternoons, family take-home dinners) that align with budget constraints without diluting culinary integrity.

withEvent.title: Money Matters: Natural Food Choices in the New Financial Reality

apiEvents.htmlDescription:
Dr. Jessica Knurick is a nutrition scientist, registered dietitian, and nationally recognized science communicator whose work bridges public health, food policy, and evidence-based nutrition. With a PhD in nutrition science and a background in chronic disease prevention and public health, she spent years in academia and clinical research before focusing on translating science for the public.

Jessica is known for breaking down health misinformation, making complex topics accessible, and helping people understand how science, food systems, and policy shape real-life choices. Her work spans research, communication, and advocacy—empowering audiences to engage with science more critically and confidently across digital platforms and public forums.

She is passionate about reshaping how expert knowledge is shared in a digital world, and believes that clear, evidence-based communication is essential to protecting public health and rebuilding trust.

withEvent.title: Friday Keynote: Dr. Jessica Knurick

apiEvents.htmlDescription:
Brands have access to an incredible amount of consumer insights — but few know how to take those insights and translate them into influencer strategies that actually move the needle. This session unpacks the full journey from understanding how and why people look to trendsetters and creators to take action, how to use those trends for influencer content that resonates with your target audience. We’ll dive into examples from Chomps, AG1, and Hungryroot – exploring  how to leverage campaign learnings to build a deeper understanding of consumer behavior and desires.

Brad Hoos, CEO of The Outloud Group will share the “why it works” behind influencer marketing, how to understand the modern consumers, and how to transition from insights to performance. He'll help you to understand how natural product brands are connecting the dots between consumer personas, key influencer verticals, and creative campaign concepts — and how influencer marketing can serve as both a storytelling engine and a source of real-time consumer data.

By viewing influencer marketing not just as a performance channel, but as a rich feedback loop that informs product, creative, and broader marketing innovations, brands can unlock value that goes far beyond traditional KPIs. Expect frameworks, fresh thinking, and real examples that show how an “influencer-first” strategy can help fuel every part of your marketing mix.

Key Takeaways:

  • Why consumers look to trendsetters and influencers to guide their decision-making
  • How to transform consumer insights into actionable influencer briefs that actually convert
  • A framework to move from consumer data → personas → influencer verticals → creative campaigns
  • How influencer programs can double as powerful consumer research and content testing engines
  • Why an “influencer-first” approach can amplify the effectiveness of other marketing channels
  • Real-world examples of natural brands using insight-led creator strategy to drive growth and deeper consumer understanding

withEvent.title: From Insights to Influence: Understanding the Modern Consumer to Run Impactful Creator Campaigns

apiEvents.htmlDescription:
We’ve all seen the charts and stats—but what if the most valuable consumer insights aren’t in the data, but in the conversations? Drawing from 30 years in the classroom and 15 years working with student leaders at Protect Our Breasts, Cynthia Barstow shares real-world stories from Gen Zs and young advocates who are redefining health, sustainability, and authenticity.  

Research tells us that the natural products industry’s core consumer skews toward the educated Northeasterner with strong values alignment. Cynthia is engaged with your next gen consumers everyday in Marketing for Health & Wellness, Sustainability Marketing and other classes.

Now, as the host of ShelfSense, a podcast connecting natural products pioneers with Gen Z voices, she reveals what these emerging shoppers actually think—and how the industry can meet them with transparency, purpose, and heart.

withEvent.title: The Value of the Anecdotal: Listening to Gen Z - Our Next-Gen Consumers

apiEvents.htmlDescription:
In today’s rapidly evolving world, wellness has become the new measure of wealth. Consumers are redefining what it means to live well, succeed, and thrive—reshaping culture, priorities, and behaviors across every generation. At the same time, we are entering an era where marketing to the algorithm, not the individual, determines visibility, influence, and growth. This presentation explores the forces reshaping the landscape and what brands must do to remain relevant.

We begin with a look at where consumers have been, where they are today, and where they’re going—highlighting the cultural shifts and emerging priorities driving the wellness-as-wealth mindset. Tech acceleration, economic pressure, and lifestyle re-evaluation have created new attributes and meanings around wellness that extend beyond physical health to emotional, financial, and digital wellbeing. Increasingly, this expanded definition of wellness is anchored in a new aspiration: longevity—the pursuit of living better, longer. Consumers are prioritizing not only health-span, but overall lifespan, viewing wellness as an integrated, long-term investment designed to extend vitality, resilience, and quality of life across the years. Amid this transformation, two major inflection points are occurring simultaneously: a generational power shift and the rise of the K-economy. Together, these shifts are influencing how consumers make decisions, how communities form, and how trust is built.

We will explore how these forces drive behavior change, influencing everything from purchasing decisions to digital engagement patterns, helping companies and brands adapt by understanding new behavioral signals, new cultural drivers, and new pathways to influence.

withEvent.title: Wellness is the New Wealth: Exploring the Shifting Consumer Outlook on Health & Wellness
